fixed_blocks.text:
III. Terms Defined

Compensatory Overtime Credits (COCs) – are any units of WL aside from actual teaching load in excess of 24 units/ term. These are pooled/lumped into compensatory time-offs (CTOS) by the HRMO that can be used by any faculty to cover his/her absences or undertime.

Seasonal/Occasional Designations – refer to designations which do not require daily and weekly client engagement, and monthly submissions of reportorial requirements and conduct of office-initiated activities.

Teaching Load (TL) refers to actual teaching loads given to faculty members measured in hours load of subjects to teach (under a. instruction) assigned to a teacher. It excludes work assignments in b) research, c) extension, d) production and e) others/administrative designations.

Illustration:

| Credit Unit(s) | Teaching Hour(s) | Teaching Load (in hours) |
|---|---|---|
| 3 (Lec) | 3 | 3 |
| 2 (Lec) | 2 | 2 |
| 1 (Lec) | 1 | 1 |

| Credit Unit(s) | Teaching Hour(s) | Teaching Load (in hours), except high- risk laboratory work exposure(s) |
|---|---|---|
| 3 (Lab) | 9 | 6.25 |
| 2 (Lab) | 6 | 4.5 |
| 1 (Lab) | 3 | 2.25 |

qa_pairs.question: What are Compensatory Overtime Credits (COCs)?
qa_pairs.answer: Compensatory Overtime Credits (COCs) refer to any units of Workload aside from actual teaching load in excess of 24 units per term. These are pooled/lumped into compensatory time-offs (CTOS) by the HRMO that can be used by any faculty member to cover absences or undertime.

qa_pairs.question: How many Seasonal/Occasional Designations can a faculty member have in a term?
qa_pairs.answer: A faculty member can have up to 3 Seasonal/Occasional Designations in a term.

qa_pairs.context:
III. Terms Defined

Compensatory Overtime Credits (COCs) – are any units of WL aside from actual teaching load in excess of 24 units/ term. These are pooled/lumped into compensatory time-offs (CTOS) by the HRMO that can be used by any faculty to cover his/her absences or undertime.

Seasonal/Occasional Designations – refer to designations which do not require daily and weekly client engagement, and monthly submissions of reportorial requirements and conduct of office-initiated activities.

qa_pairs.question: What is the difference between Teaching Load and Workload?
qa_pairs.answer: Teaching Load refers to actual teaching loads given to faculty members, while Workload refers to any units of work assigned to a faculty member beyond their teaching load.

qa_pairs.question: Can a faculty member use Compensatory Overtime Credits (COCs) to cover research, extension, production, or other administrative designations?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Compensatory Overtime Credits (COCs) can only be used to cover absences or undertime.

| 2 (Lec) | 2 | 2 |
| 1 (Lec) | 1 | 1 |

| Credit Unit(s) | Teaching Hour(s) | Teaching Load (in hours), except high- risk laboratory work exposure(s) |
|---|---|---|
| 3 (Lab) | 9 | 6.25 |
| 2 (Lab) | 6 | 4.5 |
| 1 (Lab) | 3 | 2.25 |
qa_pairs.question: What is the difference between Teaching Load and Workload for a faculty member with Seasonal/Occasional Designations?
qa_pairs.answer: For a faculty member with Seasonal/Occasional Designations, Teaching Load refers to actual teaching loads given to faculty members, while Workload refers to any units of work assigned to a faculty member beyond their teaching load.

qa_pairs.context:
III. Terms Defined

Compensatory Overtime Credits (COCs) – are any units of WL aside from actual teaching load in excess of 24 units/ term. These are pooled/lumped into compensatory time-offs (CTOS) by the HRMO that can be used by any faculty to cover his/her absences or undertime.

Seasonal/Occasional Designations – refer to designations which do not require daily and weekly client engagement, and monthly submissions of reportorial requirements and conduct of office-initiated activities.

Teaching Load (TL) refers to actual teaching loads given to faculty members measured in hours load of subjects to teach (under a. instruction) assigned to a teacher. It excludes work assignments in b) research, c) extension, d) production and e) others/administrative designations.

Illustration:

| Credit Unit(s) | Teaching Hour(s) | Teaching Load (in hours) |
|---|---|---|
| 3 (Lec) | 3 | 3 |
| 2 (Lec) | 2 | 2 |
| 1 (Lec) | 1 | 1 |

| Credit Unit(s) | Teaching Hour(s) | Teaching Load (in hours), except high- risk laboratory work exposure(s) |
|---|---|---|
| 3 (Lab) | 9 | 6.25 |
| 2 (Lab) | 6 | 4.5 |
| 1 (Lab) | 3 | 2.25 |
qa_pairs.question: Can a faculty member use Compensatory Overtime Credits (COCs) to cover absences or undertime for a faculty member with Seasonal/Occasional Designations?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, a faculty member can use Compensatory Overtime Credits (COCs) to cover absences or undertime for a faculty member with Seasonal/Occasional Designations.
